source: Original plain-English summaries written by the project author. MIT-licensed creative work.

summaries.ohms-law: Ohm's Law relates voltage, current, and resistance. Power equals voltage times current. Given any two of V, I, R, P, the others follow.
summaries.wire-ampacity: The current a conductor can carry without exceeding its insulation's temperature rating. Computed from conductor resistance, ambient temperature, and a standard heat-balance methodology.
summaries.voltage-drop: The voltage lost along a conductor due to its resistance. Express as volts and as a percentage of source voltage.
summaries.conduit-fill: The cross-sectional area occupied by conductors inside a conduit, expressed as a percentage of the conduit's interior area.
summaries.box-fill: The cubic-inch volume occupied by conductors and devices inside an electrical box.
summaries.breaker-sizing: Minimum breaker size for a load. Continuous loads require sizing at 125 percent of the load current.
summaries.motor-fla: Typical full-load amps for common motor sizes from manufacturer technical bulletins.
summaries.transformer-sizing: Required transformer kVA from a load's apparent power.
summaries.three-phase: Power, apparent power, and reactive power for a balanced three-phase load.
summaries.copper-resistance: Resistance of a copper or aluminum conductor at a given temperature, length, and gauge.
summaries.egc-sizing: Minimum equipment grounding conductor size based on the upstream overcurrent device rating.
summaries.pipe-sizing: Water supply pipe size from fixture units (Hunter's Curve method) or drainage pipe size from drainage fixture units.
summaries.friction-loss: Pressure loss along a pipe due to friction. Hazen-Williams for water; Darcy-Weisbach for gas and other fluids.
summaries.pipe-volume: Volume of fluid contained in a pipe of a given diameter and length.
summaries.pump-sizing: Required pump head and flow rate to deliver water at a target rate against the system's resistance.
summaries.static-pressure-piping: Pressure loss in a piping system from elevation and friction.
summaries.gas-pipe-sizing: Required gas pipe size for a given BTU load, run length, gas type, and allowable pressure drop.
summaries.slope: Slope expressed as fraction (1/4 inch per foot), degrees, and percent.
summaries.pressure-conversion: Bidirectional conversion between PSI, head feet, inches of water column, kPa, and bar.
summaries.backflow: Common backflow scenarios and the typical preventer types used to prevent each.
summaries.manual-j-cooling: A simplified estimator of the sensible and latent cooling load for a building. A code-compliant load calculation requires Manual J.
summaries.manual-j-heating: A simplified estimator of the heating load for a building. A code-compliant load calculation requires Manual J.
summaries.duct-sizing: Required duct size for a given airflow and friction rate.
summaries.static-pressure-hvac: Total external static pressure for an HVAC system from the sum of element pressure drops.
summaries.refrigerant-pt: Saturated pressure or temperature for a refrigerant from manufacturer-published P-T data.
summaries.superheat-subcool: Degrees of superheat or subcool from a refrigerant line temperature and the system pressure.
summaries.seer-eer: Bidirectional conversion between cooling efficiency rating systems.
summaries.balance-point: Outdoor temperature at which a heat pump's heating capacity equals the building's heat loss.
summaries.shr: Sensible heat ratio: sensible cooling load divided by total cooling load.
summaries.cfm-per-ton: Standard 400 CFM per ton airflow with adjustments for high humidity (350 CFM) and dry climate (450 CFM).
summaries.combustion-air: Required combustion air opening size for a fuel-burning appliance based on BTU input and room volume.
summaries.psychrometric: Dew point, grains per pound, vapor pressure, and specific humidity from temperature and relative humidity.
summaries.drying-goal: Target indoor grains per pound for effective structural drying based on outdoor conditions.
summaries.dehumidifier: Required dehumidifier capacity in pints per day by AHAM rating and field methods.
summaries.air-movers: Number of air movers and CFM coverage required for a given affected area and water class.
summaries.water-classes: IICRC categories 1, 2, 3 and classes 1 through 4 with original plain-English summaries.
summaries.drying-times: Typical drying times for common building materials by contamination class.
summaries.mold: Conditions under which mold growth is likely: temperature, relative humidity, and time.
summaries.ppe: Typical personal protective equipment selection by water category and contamination type.
summaries.stairs: Number of risers, riser height, run, total run, and headroom check from the total rise.
summaries.roof-pitch: Pitch as a fraction, degrees, and percent. Bidirectional inputs.
summaries.rafter: Rafter length from horizontal span, pitch, and overhang. Pythagorean.
summaries.square-footage: Area for rectangle, triangle, trapezoid, and circle.
summaries.board-footage: Total board feet for lumber from thickness, width, length, and count.
summaries.concrete: Cubic yards of concrete for slabs, footings, columns, and footings with stems.
summaries.rebar: Linear feet of rebar in a slab from dimensions, spacing on center, and edge clearance.
summaries.lumber-spans: Maximum span for common lumber sizes computed from first-principles beam mechanics and species and grade material properties.
summaries.fastener-pullout: Typical pull-out resistance for common nails and screws by wood species and length of penetration.
summaries.beam-loading: Maximum moment and deflection for simply supported beams under point and uniform loads.
summaries.material-quantity: Quantity of material for common assemblies (drywall, paint, flooring, roofing, siding) with waste factor.
summaries.fire-friction: Friction loss in fire hose using the standard fireground formula CQ^2L with coefficients per hose diameter.
summaries.pdp: Required pump discharge pressure as the sum of nozzle pressure, friction loss, elevation, and appliance loss.
summaries.hydrant-flow: Hydrant discharge in GPM from a Pitot pressure reading and outlet diameter using the standard hydrant flow formula.
summaries.required-fire-flow: Estimated needed fire flow from structure square footage, occupancy classification, and exposure factor (ISO method).
summaries.master-stream: Typical master-stream reach in feet from nozzle pressure and nozzle type.
summaries.aerial-ladder: Horizontal and vertical reach of an aerial ladder from set angle and extension length.
summaries.foam: Required foam concentrate volume from fire size, application rate, and foam concentrate percentage.
summaries.smoke-reading: Plain-English reference for interpreting smoke volume, velocity, density, and color.
summaries.unit-converter: Bidirectional conversion across length, area, volume, mass, force, pressure, temperature, energy, power, flow, and electrical units.
summaries.material-cost: Total cost from price per unit and quantity, with optional tax and delivery.
summaries.markup: Selling price from cost and a target markup or margin.
summaries.time-and-materials: Total billable amount from labor hours, labor rate, and material cost.
summaries.sales-tax: Tax amount and total from a state and a subtotal using bundled state revenue department published rates.
summaries.tip-out: Per-person split of revenue across a crew using each member's hours.
